surface.name: Get started with Exa via cURL Search
surface.selection_basis: Exa's Get started guide states its outcome is making the first request to an Exa API endpoint. The same page documents a cURL POST to https://api.exa.ai/search with x-api-key and says cURL needs no installation. Search and crawl is the first listed use case. SDK install is optional.

alternatives_considered.name: Python or JavaScript SDK Search
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Optional client libraries; the quickstart documents an equivalent cURL Search request and says cURL needs no installation.

alternatives_considered.name: Answer or Chat Completions first request
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Peer use cases on the same page; Search and crawl is listed first for the first request.

alternatives_considered.name: API Playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Separate exploration route; QuickStart defines first success as an API request.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create an Exa account, obtain free credits and an API key, and complete a Search API request over HTTPS without installing an SDK.
assumed_prior_state: No Exa account, free credits, API key, or prior request is assumed.
A terminal with cURL is available; no language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated cURL POST to https://api.exa.ai/search returns search results JSON.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Search API returns a successful JSON body containing search results for the requested query.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Get started with Exa documents Make your first request, includes a cURL POST to https://api.exa.ai/search with x-api-key, and states cURL needs no installation.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The first meaningful Exa success is a successful authenticated Search API response. cURL is documented on the same quickstart as the SDKs, and the page says cURL needs no installation. New accounts receive $10 free credits after onboarding; a payment card is not required for that first funded request while free credits remain.

prerequisites.requirement: An Exa account that can open the Dashboard.
A positive credit balance. New accounts receive $10 in free credits when onboarding is completed; requests are blocked when the balance runs out.
An Exa API key from the Dashboard, exported as EXA_API_KEY.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client). No language SDK is required.

details: The page states Make your first request to one of Exa's API endpoints.
primary_path.output: Documented first-request path with cURL and SDK tabs
primary_path.success_signal: The cURL Search example is visible.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to an Exa account and open the Dashboard.
details: Treat signup as one opaque step at the official Dashboard entry.
primary_path.input: Intent to use the Exa API
primary_path.success_signal: API Keys and Billing are reachable.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the displayed account recovery path.

primary_path.action: Complete new-account onboarding to receive the documented $10 in free credits.
details: Billing docs state new accounts receive $10 in free credits when you complete onboarding.
A payment method is not required for this free-credit first request while the balance remains positive.
primary_path.success_signal: Billing shows a positive credit balance from free credits.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Incomplete onboarding leaves credits unavailable; exhausted credits block API requests until more credits are added.

primary_path.action: Get an Exa API key from the Dashboard and store it securely.
details: The quickstart says to get the key from the Exa Dashboard.
primary_path.success_signal: The key value is available locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No manual approval wait is documented for key creation.

primary_path.action: Export the key as EXA_API_KEY in the terminal.
details: macOS/Linux: export EXA_API_KEY=...; Windows: setx EXA_API_KEY ...
primary_path.output: Shell environment ready for authenticated HTTP
primary_path.success_signal: EXA_API_KEY is set for the current shell.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Shell persistence depends on the OS.

primary_path.action: POST a Search request with cURL to https://api.exa.ai/search.
details: Include accept and content-type application/json headers.
Send a JSON body with query, type auto, and contents.highlights as shown in the quickstart cURL Search example.
primary_path.success_signal: The API returns a successful JSON body containing search results.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing key, exhausted credits (402), or invalid JSON produce API errors.

primary_path.action: Confirm the Search JSON includes search results for the query.
primary_path.success_signal: Search results are present in the successful response.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An error body means the first call did not succeed.

branches.condition: The developer prefers the Python or JavaScript SDK.
branches.path: Install exa-py or exa-js (and dotenv if using a .env file), then run the published Search example with the same EXA_API_KEY and credit balance.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds optional local package installation without changing the authenticated Search boundary.

branches.condition: The developer prefers Answer or Chat Completions instead of Search.
branches.path: Use the documented cURL examples for https://api.exa.ai/answer or https://api.exa.ai/chat/completions with the same API key.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Peer first-request endpoints on the same quickstart page.

branches.condition: Free credits are exhausted before or during the first request.
branches.path: Add credits from Billing (payment via Stripe) or enable auto recharge before retrying the API call.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Billing purchase becomes required once the free balance is gone.

friction_gates.description: An Exa account and Dashboard access are required before an API key can be created.
New accounts must complete onboarding to receive the documented $10 free credits; requests fail when credits are exhausted.
A secret API key must be obtained and supplied as x-api-key on the Search request.

sources.title: Get started with Exa
evidence_supported: cURL POST to https://api.exa.ai/search with x-api-key

sources.title: Exa Dashboard
evidence_supported: Dashboard entry for account and keys

sources.title: Error codes
evidence_supported: Requests fail with 402 when credits are exhausted

sources.title: Billing
evidence_supported: Requests blocked when credit balance runs out
Add credits via Stripe when needed

uncertainties.question: Exact interactive fields and steps inside Dashboard onboarding that gate the $10 free credits.
uncertainties.impact: Could add undocumented form or wait steps before free credits appear.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Billing docs state free credits after onboarding but do not publish the interactive onboarding field contract.

excluded_after_success: Answer and Chat Completions as alternate first endpoints
Auto recharge and purchased credits after free balance
